generatedSentence: Commander Kael, a vigilant warrior with a `freckle` on his cheek, meticulously reviewed `accounts` in his `headset`. His `girlfriend`, an `interesting` but `flakey` `eyeglass` designer, often found his `aerobic` routines `bright`. He championed `honesty` in the `industrial` `academy`'s `economy`, a system often `empty` of `calories` for its workers, seeking to `clarify` any potential `backfire` from the new `fanjet` designs, ensuring they were `foolproof`. He knew that `inter`-departmental `heights` could `include` a `beetle` in the system, but his commander instincts remained `bright` in the `drizzle`. A `major` `inferno` flared, threatening to `interrupt` the city's `jubilee`. Whispers spread through the `networks` of an impending `lockdown` by a cruel `jailer` `jockeying` for power. Kael recalled a forgotten `journal` entry describing `magic` `kabobs` used by ancient `kidders` `kipping` in the `kennel` district, capable of a `major` `upthrow`. The `latest` `missile` prototypes, intended for target `nocking`, were `none` of them `foolproof`. The `joblessness` had `jumped`, creating `mischief` as `liters` of raw `oxides` were `knocked` from the `gate`. The `commander` `jumped`, propelled by an `updraft`, to `sanctify` the `throne` from the `cannibal` `gang`. Below, `giddyup` `visitors` with `heartburn` `wined` and `dined` as the `government` tried to `clarify` the `outcry`. A `raw` `fragment` of `obsidian` armor, a `vestige` of ancient `warrior` `retribution`, deflected a `uniform` `missile` designed to `biffed` him. The `unsightly` `downpour` turned `smooth` as `oils` coated the arena. He `knocked` a `tough` `dingo` back, its `beehive` fur matted with unbaked `marshmallows`. Through the `nether` `transmission`, a `feminist` `antifa` `major` `wins` `reassurance`, `finding` the `prophecy` fulfilled. The `cannibal` `gang` was `bricked` into their own `jail`, `stoned` by the irony. Kael, though `guilty` of some `mischief` in his youth, had `eagerly` secured the `harvest`. His `journal` `accounts` could now `clarify` the complex `taxonomy` of the `omnivorous` `beetle` that almost backfired on the `sail` to freedom. His `incandescent` spirit, no longer burdened by `ordinary` `calorie` counts, felt `complete` in the `new` beginning.

His `girlfriend`, an `interesting` but `flakey` `eyeglass` designer, often found his `aerobic` routines `bright`. He championed `honesty` in the `industrial` `academy`'s `economy`, a system often `empty` of `calories` for its workers, seeking to `clarify` any potential `backfire` from the new `fanjet` designs, ensuring they were `foolproof`. Kael recalled a forgotten `journal` entry describing `magic` `kabobs` used by ancient `kidders` `kipping` in the `kennel` district, capable of a `major` `upthrow`. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. PROTAGONIST: A lean, cybernetic warrior with glowing blue data-stream tattoos and a silver chrome helmet with a single glowing red eyeglass. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Commander Kael reviews data on a holographic interface with a serious expression, his cybernetic helmet reflecting an industrial skyline, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An aerial view of a futuristic city in chaos, a massive inferno blazing in the distance, with small figures (representing the 'gang') scrambling below, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Commander Kael mid-air, deflecting a missile with an obsidian fragment on his gauntlet, a snarling dingo-like creature at his feet, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist Commander Kael standing triumphant amidst a revitalized city, looking towards a setting sun, a sense of peace and resolution, wide shot.

definitions.definition: The metric unit of fluid measure, equal to one cubic decimetre. Symbols: l, L, ℓ
definitions.example: You should be able to fill four cups with one litre of water.

definitions.definition: A measure of volume equivalent to a litre.
An organism which eats others of its own species or kind, especially a human who eats human flesh.
A diary or daily record of a person, organization, vessel etc.; daybook.
A newspaper or magazine dealing with a particular subject.
definitions.example: The university's biology department subscribes to half a dozen academic journals.

definitions.definition: A chronological record of payments.
A chronological record of changes made to a database or other system; along with a backup or image copy that allows recovery after a failure or reinstatement to a previous time; a log.
The part of a shaft or axle that rests on bearings.
To archive or record something.
To scrapbook.
To insert (a shaft, etc.) in a journal bearing.
To bury in a grave.
To confine, as in a prison.
An upward current of air, especially a strong one
Something that is smooth, or that goes smoothly and easily.
A smoothing action.
A domestic animal having a smooth coat.
A member of an anti-hippie fashion movement in 1970s Britain.
The analysis obtained through a smoothing procedure.
To make smooth or even.
To make straightforward or easy.
To calm or palliate.
definitions.example: to smooth a person's temper

definitions.definition: (image processing, digital audio) To capture important patterns in the data, while leaving out noise.
To stroke; especially to stroke an animal's fur.
definitions.example: Can I smooth your cat?

definitions.definition: Having a texture that lacks friction. Not rough.
Without difficulty, problems, or unexpected consequences or incidents.
definitions.example: We hope for a smooth transition to the new system.

definitions.definition: Bland; glib.
Flowing or uttered without check, obstruction, or hesitation; not harsh; fluent.
(of a person) Suave; sophisticated.
(of an action) Natural; unconstrained.
(of a motion) Unbroken.
(chiefly of water) Placid, calm.
(of an edge) Lacking projections or indentations; not serrated.
(of food or drink) Not grainy; having an even texture.
(of a beverage) Having a pleasantly rounded flavor; neither rough nor astringent.
(of a function) Having derivatives of all finite orders at all points within the function’s domain.
(of a number) That factors completely into small prime numbers.
(of a vowel) Lacking marked aspiration.
(of muscles) Involuntary and non-striated.
Smoothly.
A small, controlled fire set in the path of a larger uncontrolled fire, in order to limit the spread of the large fire by removing its fuel.

An explosion produced either by a running internal combustion engine that occurs in the air intake or exhaust system rather than inside the combustion chamber or unburned fuel or hydrocarbons ignited somewhere in the exhaust system.
A premature explosion in the cylinder of a gas or oil engine during the exhaust or the compression stroke, tending to drive the piston in the wrong direction.
An explosion in the exhaust passages of an internal combustion engine.
(of a gun, cannon, Bunsen burner, etc.) To fire in the opposite direction, for example due to an obstruction in the barrel.
(of an engine) To experience a premature ignition of fuel or an ignition of exhaust gases, making a popping sound.
To fail in a manner that brings down further misfortune.
definitions.example: His attempt to make money by importing luxury cars backfired horribly when fuel prices tripled.

definitions.definition: An artist's brush used in oil and acrylic painting with a long ferrule and a flat, somewhat tapering bristle head.
Splendour; brightness
A person with a naturalistic worldview with no supernatural or mystical elements.
(in the plural) The high-beam intensity of motor vehicle headlamps.
definitions.example: Your brights are on.

definitions.definition: Visually dazzling; luminous, lucent, clear, radiant; not dark.
definitions.example: Could you please dim the light? It's far too bright.

definitions.definition: Having a clear, quick intellect; intelligent.
definitions.example: He's very bright. He was able to solve the problem without my help.

definitions.definition: Vivid, colourful, brilliant.
definitions.example: The orange and blue walls of the sitting room were much brighter than the dull grey walls of the kitchen.

definitions.definition: Happy, in good spirits.
definitions.example: I woke up today feeling so bright that I decided to have a little dance.

definitions.definition: Sparkling with wit; lively; vivacious; cheerful.
Illustrious; glorious.
Clear; transparent.
Manifest to the mind, as light is to the eyes; clear; evident; plain.
An impressive seat used by a monarch, often on a raised dais in a throne room and reserved for formal occasions.
definitions.example: He approached the throne reverently.

definitions.definition: Leadership, particularly the position of a monarch.
The seat of a bishop in the cathedral-church of his diocese; also, the seat of a pope.
Other seats, particularly:
A member of an order of angels ranked above dominions and below cherubim.
To place on a royal seat; to enthrone.
To place in an elevated position; to give sovereignty or dominion to; to exalt.
To be in, or sit upon, a throne; to be placed as if upon a throne.
Light rain.
Very small, numerous, and uniformly dispersed water drops, mist, or sprinkle. Unlike fog droplets, drizzle falls to the ground.
definitions.example: No longer pouring, the rain outside slowed down to a faint drizzle.

definitions.definition: Water.
definitions.example: Stop drinking all of my drizzle!

definitions.definition: A cake onto which icing, honey or syrup has been drizzled in an artistic manner.
To rain lightly.
definitions.example: We had planned a picnic for Joe's birthday, but it ended up drizzling all day.

definitions.definition: To shed slowly in minute drops or particles.
To pour slowly and evenly, especially oil or honey in cooking.
definitions.example: The recipe says to toss the salad and then drizzle olive oil on it.

definitions.definition: To cover by pouring in this manner.
definitions.example: The recipe says to toss the salad and then drizzle it in olive oil.

definitions.definition: To urinate.
To carry out parfilage, the process of unravelling.
A heavy rain.
definitions.example: They got caught in a downpour without an umbrella and came back soaked.

definitions.definition: To pour down; rain heavily.
The feeling of being reassured, of having confidence restored, of having apprehensions dispelled.
The act of confirming someone's opinion or impression.
Reinsurance.
A small brownish or reddish pigmentation spot on the surface of the skin.
definitions.example: Steve has brown hair, blue eyes, and freckles on his cheeks and nose.

definitions.definition: Any small spot or discoloration.
A small sweet consisting of a flattish mound of chocolate covered in hundreds and thousands.
The anus.
To cover with freckles.
To become covered with freckles.
A person with authority; authority, ordinance.
Something ordinary or regular.
(of a judge) Having regular jurisdiction; now only used in certain phrases.
Being part of the natural order of things; normal, customary, routine.
definitions.example: On an ordinary day I wake up at nine o'clock, work for six hours, and then go to the gym.

definitions.definition: Having no special characteristics or function; everyday, common, mundane; often deprecatory.
definitions.example: He looked so ordinary, I never thought he'd be capable of murder.

definitions.definition: Bad or undesirable.
Conduct that playfully causes petty annoyance.
definitions.example: Drink led to mischief.

definitions.definition: A playfully annoying action.
definitions.example: John's mischief, tying his shoelaces together, irked George at first.

definitions.definition: (collective) A group or a pack of rats.
Harm or injury:
A cause or agent of annoyance, harm or injury, especially a person who causes mischief.
The Devil; used as an expletive.
A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.
A sum of money deposited at a bank and subject to withdrawal.
definitions.example: to keep one's account at the bank.

definitions.definition: A statement in general of reasons, causes, grounds, etc., explanatory of some event; a reason of an action to be done.
definitions.example: No satisfactory account has been given of these phenomena.

definitions.definition: A reason, grounds, consideration, motive; a person's sake.
definitions.example: Don't trouble yourself on my account.

definitions.definition: A business relationship involving the exchange of money and credit.
A record of events; recital of transactions; a relation or narrative; a report; a description.
definitions.example: An account of a battle.

definitions.definition: An estimate or estimation; valuation; judgment.
Importance; worth; value; esteem; judgement.
An authorization to use a service.
definitions.example: I've opened an account with Wikipedia so that I can contribute and partake in the project.

definitions.definition: A reckoning; computation; calculation; enumeration; a record of some reckoning.
Profit; advantage.
To provide explanation.
To count.
The distance from the base of something to the top.
The vertical distance from the ground to the highest part of a standing person or animal (withers in the case of a horse).
The highest point or maximum degree.
definitions.example: She's at the height of her career.

definitions.definition: A high point.
An area of land at the top of a cliff.
The act, quality, or condition of being honest.
definitions.example: academic / artistic / emotional / intellectual honesty

definitions.definition: Honor; decency, propriety.
Chastity.
Any of various crucifers in the genus Lunaria, several of which are grown as ornamentals, particularly Lunaria annua.
A pair of headphones or earphones, or a singular headphone or earphone, typically with an attached microphone.
Any electronic device worn on the head.
On a bicycle, the system of bearings that connects the fork to the frame.
(Jewish history) A special year of emancipation supposed to be kept every fifty years, when farming was abandoned and Hebrew slaves were set free.
A 25th, 40th, 50th, 60th or 70th anniversary.
A special year (originally held every hundred years, then fifty, and then fewer) in which remission from sin could be granted as well as indulgences upon making a pilgrimage to Rome.
A time of celebration or rejoicing.
An occasion of mass manumission from slavery.
A period of fifty years; a half-century.
(19th-mid 20th century) An employee in industry.
An enterprise producing tangible goods or providing certain services to industrial companies.
A bond or stock issued by such a company.
A film made for use within an industry, not for a movie-going audience.
Industrial music.
definitions.example: I wish they'd play more industrial in this club.

definitions.definition: An industrial piercing.
Of or relating to industry, notably manufacturing.
definitions.example: The industrial segment of the economy has seen troubles lately.

definitions.definition: Produced by such industry.
definitions.example: Handicraft is less standardized then industrial products, hence less artistic or rather flawless.

definitions.definition: Used by such industry.
Suitable for use in such industry; industrial-grade.
definitions.example: This is an industrial product—it's much too strong for home use.

definitions.definition: Massive in scale or quantity.
Employed as manpower by such industry.
(of a society or country) Having many industries; industrialized.
definitions.example: Italy is a part-industrial, part-rural nation.

definitions.definition: Belonging or pertaining to the genre of industrial music.
definitions.example: a track with clashing industrial beats

definitions.definition: A female partner in an unmarried romantic relationship.
definitions.example: Marc went to the park with his girlfriend and watched the sunset with her.

definitions.definition: A female friend.
definitions.example: Mary always enjoyed hanging out with her girlfriend Jessica.

definitions.definition: A plea by a defendant who does not contest a charge.
A verdict of a judge or jury on a defendant judged to have committed a crime.
One who is declared guilty of a crime.
Responsible for a dishonest act.
definitions.example: He was guilty of cheating at cards.

definitions.definition: Judged to have committed a crime.
definitions.example: The guilty man was led away.

definitions.definition: Having a sense of guilt.
definitions.example: Do you have a guilty conscience?

definitions.definition: Blameworthy.
definitions.example: I have a guilty secret.

definitions.definition: A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along. The sail may be attached to the boat via a combination of mast, spars and ropes.
(nautical,uncountable) The concept of a sail or sails, as if a substance.
definitions.example: Take in sail: a storm is coming.

definitions.definition: The power harnessed by a sail or sails, or the use of this power for travel or transport.
A trip in a boat, especially a sailboat.
definitions.example: Let's go for a sail.

definitions.definition: (plural "sail") A sailing vessel; a vessel of any kind; a craft.
definitions.example: Twenty sail were in sight.

definitions.definition: The blade of a windmill.
A tower-like structure found on the dorsal (topside) surface of submarines.
The floating organ of siphonophores, such as the Portuguese man-of-war.
A sailfish.
definitions.example: We caught three sails today.

definitions.definition: An outward projection of the spine, occurring in certain dinosaurs and synapsids
Anything resembling a sail, such as a wing.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: To punch or hit.
To discard; to throw out; to throw away.
To wipe out; to faceplant; to fall.
A 12- to 13-year-old participant in the Young Women organization of the LDS Church.
An enclosed structure in which some species of honey bees (genus Apis) live and raise their young.
A man-made structure in which bees are kept for their honey.
Any place full of activity, or in which people are very busy.
A women's hairstyle, popular in the 1960s, in which long hair is styled into a hive-shaped form on top of the head and usually held in place with lacquer.
A particular style of hat.
A type of anti-personnel ammunition round containing flechettes, and characterised by the buzzing sound made as they fly through the air.
In Conway's Game of Life, a particular still life configuration with a rounded appearance.
To fill (a place) with busy activity.
Canis lupus dingo, a wild dog native to Australia.
Effective management of a community or system, or especially its resources.
The study of money, currency and trade, and the efficient use of resources.
Frugal use of resources.
definitions.example: economy of word

definitions.definition: The system of production and distribution and consumption. The overall measure of a currency system; as the national economy.
The method of divine government of the world. (See w:Economy (religion).)
The part of a commercial passenger airplane or train reserved for those paying the lower standard fares; economy class.
Management of one’s residency.
Cheap to run; using minimal resources; representing good value for money.
definitions.example: Economy size.

definitions.definition: (sugar refining, sugar trade) An unprocessed sugar; a batch of such.
A galled place; an inveterate sore.
(by extension) A point about which a person is particularly sensitive.
A recording or rip of a show that has not been fansubbed.
A scan that has not been cleaned (purged of blemishes arising from the scanning process) and has not been scanlated.
(of food) Not cooked.
(of materials, products, etc.) Not treated or processed; in a natural state, unrefined, unprocessed.
definitions.example: raw cane sugar

definitions.definition: Having had the skin removed or abraded; chafed, tender; exposed, lacerated.
definitions.example: a raw wound

definitions.definition: New or inexperienced.
definitions.example: a raw beginner

definitions.definition: Crude in quality; rough, uneven, unsophisticated.
definitions.example: a raw voice

definitions.definition: (of data) Uncorrected, without analysis.
(of weather) Unpleasantly cold or damp.
definitions.example: a raw wind

definitions.definition: (of an emotion, personality, etc.) Unmasked, undisguised, strongly expressed
definitions.example: raw emotion

definitions.definition: Candid in a representation of unpleasant facts, conditions, etc.
definitions.example: a raw description of the American political arena

definitions.definition: (of language) Unrefined, crude, or insensitive, especially with reference to sexual matters
Not covered; bare; bald.
Without a condom.
definitions.example: We did it raw.

definitions.definition: A turbofan engine.
An airplane powered by turbofan engines.
Consisting of flakes or of small, loose masses; lying, or cleaving off, in flakes or layers; flakelike.
(of a person) Unreliable; likely to make plans with others but then abandon those plans.
(of a thing) Unreliable; working only on an intermittent basis; likely to malfunction.
definitions.example: I cannot enjoy the online game because of my flaky Internet connection.

definitions.definition: An advocate of feminism; a person who believes in bringing about the equality of the sexes (of women and men) in all aspects of public and private life
A member of a feminist political movement
Relating to or in accordance with feminism.
A part broken off; a small, detached portion; an imperfect part, either physically or not
definitions.example: I heard a small fragment of the conversation.

definitions.definition: (grammar) A sentence not containing a subject or a predicate.
An incomplete portion of code.
A portion of a URL referring to a subordinate resource (such as a specific point on a web page), introduced by the # sign.
To break apart.
To cause to be broken into pieces.
To break up and disperse (a file) into non-contiguous areas of a disk.
Liquid fat.
Petroleum-based liquid used as fuel or lubricant.
An oil painting.
Oil paint.
definitions.example: I prefer to paint in oil

definitions.definition: To lubricate with oil.
To grease with oil for cooking.
A loud cry or uproar.
definitions.example: His appearance was greeted with an outcry of jeering.

definitions.definition: A strong protest.
definitions.example: The proposal was met with a public outcry.

definitions.definition: An auction.
definitions.example: to send goods to an outcry

definitions.definition: To cry out.
To cry louder than.
(chiefly UK) To sleep; often with the connotation of a temporary or charitable situation, or one borne out of necessity.
definitions.example: Don’t worry, I’ll kip on the sofabed.

definitions.definition: To snatch; take up hastily; filch
To hold or keep (together)
To conduct oneself; act
To perform the kip maneuver.
A dish of pieces of meat, fish, or vegetables roasted on a skewer or spit, especially a doner kebab.
A shish kebab or any other food on a skewer.
The outward growing portions of a shish kebab structure.
A Muslim, usually of southern European, Middle Eastern, or North African descent.
The vulva.
To roast in the style of a kebab
To stab or skewer
One who kids, or teases light-heartedly.
A term of endearment/address for a child or young adult, usually male.
A forestaller or huckster.
A kidderminster (carpet)
Any object used as a weapon by being thrown or fired through the air, such as stone, arrow or bullet.
definitions.example: The Rhodians, who used leaden bullets, were able to project their missiles twice as far as the Persian slingers, who used large stones.

definitions.definition: A self-propelled projectile whose trajectory can be adjusted after it is launched.
definitions.example: That missile is explosive enough to kill hundreds.

definitions.definition: Living or occurring only in the presence of oxygen e.g. aerobic bacteria.
Of exercise, performed while maintaining a sufficient supply of oxygen to meet bodily energy needs.
Of or relating to aerobics.
A decentralised, radical political movement that opposes fascism.
A local instance of this movement.
Opposition to fascism
A person opposed to the tenets of fascism.
Opposed to the tenets of fascism; antifa.
The third season of the year; autumn; fall.
definitions.example: Harvest is usually very damp and rainy.

definitions.definition: The season of gathering ripened crops; specifically, the time of reaping and gathering grain.
The process of gathering the ripened crop; harvesting.
The yield of harvesting, i.e., the gathered crops or fruits.
definitions.example: This year's cotton harvest was great but the corn harvest was disastrous.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The product or result of any exertion or course of action; reward or consequences.
A modern pagan ceremony held on or around the autumn equinox, which is in the harvesting season.
To bring in a harvest; reap; glean.
To be occupied bringing in a harvest
definitions.example: Harvesting is a stressing, thirsty occupation

definitions.definition: To win, achieve a gain.
definitions.example: The rising star harvested well-deserved acclaim, even an Oscar under 21

definitions.definition: Near the end of a period of time.
definitions.example: It was late in the evening when we finally arrived.

definitions.definition: Specifically, near the end of the day.
definitions.example: It was getting late and I was tired.

definitions.definition: (usually not comparable) Associated with the end of a period.
definitions.example: Late Latin is less fully inflected than classical Latin.

definitions.definition: Not arriving until after an expected time.
definitions.example: Even though we drove as fast as we could, we were still late.

definitions.definition: Not having had an expected menstrual period.
definitions.example: I'm late, honey. Could you buy a test?

definitions.definition: Deceased, dead: used particularly when speaking of the dead person's actions while alive. (Often used with "the"; see usage notes.)
definitions.example: Her late husband had left her well provided for.

definitions.definition: Existing or holding some position not long ago, but not now; departed, or gone out of office.
definitions.example: the late administration

definitions.definition: Recent — relative to the noun it modifies.
Of a star or class of stars, cooler than the sun.
After a deadline has passed, past a designated time.
definitions.example: We drove as fast as we could, but we still arrived late.

definitions.definition: Formerly, especially in the context of service in a military unit.
definitions.example: Colonel Easterwood, late of the 34th Carbines, was a guest at the dinner party.

definitions.definition: Not long ago; just now.
The most recent thing, particularly information or news.
definitions.example: Have you heard the latest?

definitions.definition: (of liquids, such as wine or syrup) To make clear or bright by freeing from feculent matter
To make clear or easily understood; to explain in order to remove doubt or obscurity
To grow or become clear or transparent; to become free from feculent impurities, as wine or other liquid under clarification.
definitions.example: Leave the wine for 24 hours and it will clarify.

definitions.definition: To grow clear or bright; to clear up.
To glorify.
The state of being jobless or unemployed
The phenomenon or level of unemployment in an economy
To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
Watchful, especially for danger or disorder; alert; wary
definitions.example: Be vigilant for signs of disease in your garden.

definitions.definition: A place or situation resembling Hell.
A large fire, a conflagration.
An event that causes a computer or other device to temporarily cease what it was doing and attend to a condition.
definitions.example: The interrupt caused the packet handler routine to run.

definitions.definition: To disturb or halt (an ongoing process or action, or the person performing it) by interfering suddenly.
definitions.example: A maverick politician repeatedly interrupted the debate by shouting.

definitions.definition: To divide; to separate; to break the monotony of.
definitions.example: The evenness of the road was not interrupted by a single hill.

definitions.definition: To assert to (a computer) that an exceptional condition must be handled.
definitions.example: The packet receiver circuit interrupted the microprocessor.

definitions.definition: A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
Phonetic equivalent for the letter U in the ICAO spelling alphabet, informally known as the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A uniformed police officer (as opposed to a detective).
To clothe in a uniform.
Unvarying; all the same.
Consistent; conforming to one standard.
With speed of convergence not depending on choice of function argument; as in uniform continuity, uniform convergence
(of a polymer) Composed of a single macromolecular species.
(of a polyhedron) That is isogonal and whose faces are regular polygons; (of an n-dimensional (n>3) polytope) that is isogonal and whose bounding (n-1)-dimensional facets are uniform polytopes.
Displeasing to the eye.
Not baked or cooked.
A fault in which a mass of material has been thrown up from below.
To throw or cast upwards.
To throw up (a mass of material) from below, causing a fault.
(of a mass of material) To be thrown up from below, causing a fault.
A small car, the Volkswagen Beetle (original version made 1938–2003, similar models made 1997–2010 and since 2011)
Any of numerous species of insect in the order Coleoptera characterized by a pair of hard, shell-like front wings which cover and protect a pair of rear wings when at rest.
A game of chance in which players attempt to complete a drawing of a beetle, different dice rolls allowing them to add the various body parts.
To move away quickly, to scurry away.
definitions.example: He beetled off on his vacation.

definitions.definition: The act of transmitting, e.g. data or electric power.
The fact of being transmitted.
Something that is transmitted, such as a message, picture or a disease; the sending of such a thing.
The passage of a nerve impulse across synapses.
An assembly of gears through which power is transmitted from the engine to the driveshaft in a motor car / automobile; a gearbox.

The right possessed by an heir or legatee of transmitting to his successor(s) any inheritance, legacy, right, or privilege, to which he is entitled, even if he should die without enjoying or exercising it.
(medicine, biology) The passing of a communicable disease from an infected host individual or group to a conspecific individual or group.
A person who obtains things by force; a thug or bully.
definitions.example: They were doing fine until they encountered a bunch of toughs from the opposition.

definitions.definition: To endure.
To toughen.
Strong and resilient; sturdy.
definitions.example: The tent, made of tough canvas, held up to many abuses.

definitions.definition: (of food) Difficult to cut or chew.
definitions.example: To soften a tough cut of meat, the recipe suggested simmering it for hours.

definitions.definition: Rugged or physically hardy.
definitions.example: Only a tough species will survive in the desert.

definitions.definition: Stubborn.
definitions.example: He had a reputation as a tough negotiator.

definitions.definition: (of weather etc) Harsh or severe.
Rowdy or rough.
definitions.example: A bunch of the tough boys from the wrong side of the tracks threatened him.

definitions.definition: (of questions, etc.) Difficult or demanding.
definitions.example: This is a tough crowd.

definitions.definition: Undergoing plastic deformation before breaking.
Used to indicate lack of sympathy
definitions.example: If you don't like it, tough!

definitions.definition: A prediction, especially one made by a prophet or under divine inspiration.
definitions.example: French writer Nostradamus made a prophecy in his book.

definitions.definition: The public interpretation of Scripture.
To speak or write with divine inspiration; to act as prophet.
To predict, to foretell (with or without divine inspiration).
To foreshow; to herald; to prefigure.
To speak out on the Bible as an expression of holy inspiration; to preach.
(usually plural) A container, especially a bottle, whose contents have been used up, leaving it empty.
definitions.example: Put the empties out to be recycled.

definitions.definition: To make empty; to void; to remove the contents of.
definitions.example: The cinema emptied quickly after the end of the film.

definitions.definition: Of a river, duct, etc: to drain or flow toward an ultimate destination.
definitions.example: Salmon River empties on the W shore about 2 miles below Bear River.

definitions.definition: Devoid of content; containing nothing or nobody; vacant.
definitions.example: an empty purse; an empty jug; an empty stomach

definitions.definition: Containing no elements (as of a string, array, or set), opposed to being null (having no valid value).
Free; clear; devoid; often with of.
Having nothing to carry, emptyhanded; unburdened.
Destitute of effect, sincerity, or sense; said of language.
definitions.example: empty offer

definitions.definition: Unable to satisfy; hollow; vain.
definitions.example: empty pleasures

definitions.definition: Destitute of reality, or real existence; unsubstantial.
definitions.example: empty dreams

definitions.definition: Destitute of, or lacking, sense, knowledge, or courtesy.
definitions.example: empty brains; an empty coxcomb

definitions.definition: (of some female animals, especially cows and sheep) Not pregnant; not producing offspring when expected to do so during the breeding season.
definitions.example: Empty cow rates have increased in recent years.

definitions.definition: Producing nothing; unfruitful; said of a plant or tree.
definitions.example: an empty vine

definitions.definition: In an eager manner.
To make foolproof.
definitions.example: We foolproofed the operations.

definitions.definition: For a device: protected, or designed to be proof against misuse or error.
For an idea or plan: infallible, or bulletproof.
To fit an arrow against the bowstring of a bow or crossbow. (See also notch.)
To cut a nock in (usually in an arrow's base or the tips of a bow).
A person without religious affiliation.
To no extent, in no way.
definitions.example: I felt none the worse for my recent illness.

definitions.definition: Not at all, not very.
definitions.example: He was none too pleased with the delays in the program that was supposed to be his legacy.

definitions.definition: No, not.
Not any of a given number or group.
definitions.example: None of this meat tastes right.

definitions.definition: A binary chemical compound of oxygen with another chemical element.
To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.
definitions.example: Don’t knock it until you’ve tried it.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.
definitions.example: I accidentally knocked my drink off the bar.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.
definitions.example: Knock on the door and find out if they’re home.

definitions.definition: A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.
definitions.example: He used to be a major in the army.

definitions.definition: The application of rituals or actions, especially those based on occult knowledge, to subdue or manipulate natural or supernatural beings and forces in order to have some benefit from them
A specific ritual or procedure associated with such magic; a spell.
The supernatural forces which are drawn on in such a ritual
Something producing successful and remarkable results, especially when not fully understood; an enchanting quality; exceptional skill.
A conjuring trick or illusion performed to give the appearance of supernatural phenomena or powers.
The art or practice of performing conjuring tricks and illusions.
Complicated or esoteric code that is not expected to be generally understood.
To produce, transform (something), (as if) by magic.
Having supernatural talents, properties or qualities attributed to magic.
Producing extraordinary results, as though through the use of magic; wonderful, amazing.
definitions.example: a magic moment

definitions.definition: Pertaining to conjuring tricks or illusions performed for entertainment etc.
definitions.example: a magic show; a magic trick

definitions.definition: Great; excellent.
definitions.example: — I cleaned up the flat while you were out. — Really? Magic!

definitions.definition: Describing the number of nucleons in a particularly stable isotopic nucleus; 2, 8, 20, 28, 50, 82, 126, and 184.
Being a literal number or string value with no meaning or context, not defined as a constant or variable
definitions.example: The code is full of magic numbers and we can't figure out what they mean.

definitions.definition: A person who is actively engaged in battle, conflict or warfare; a soldier or combatant.
A person who is aggressively, courageously, or energetically involved in an activity, such as athletics.

The confinement of people in their own rooms (e.g. in a school) or cells (in a prison), or to their own homes or areas (e.g. in the case of a city- or nation-wide issue) as a security measure after or amid a disturbance or pandemic, etc.
A contrivance to fasten logs together in rafting.
(nutrition) Kilogram calorie or large calorie. A unit of energy 1000 times larger than the gram calorie. It is equivalent to the gram kilocalorie, approximately 4.2 kilojoules.

The gram calorie or small calorie, a non-SI unit of energy, equivalent to approximately 4.2 joules. This unit was widely used in chemistry and physics, being the amount of energy needed to raise the temperature of 1 gram of water by 1 °C.
One who enforces confinement in a jail or prison.
To ride (a horse) in a race.
To jostle by riding against.
definitions.example: They were jockeying for position toward the end of the race.

definitions.definition: To maneuver (something) by skill for one's advantage.
To cheat or trick.
The act of one who jockeys.
Someone who visits someone else; someone staying as a guest.
Someone who pays a visit to a specific place or event; a sightseer or tourist.
(usually in the plural) Someone, or a team, that is playing away from home.
A person authorized to visit an institution to see that it is being managed properly.
An extraterrestrial being on Earth for any reason.
An object which lands or passes by Earth or its orbit.
A head or overseer of an institution such as a college (in which case, equivalent to the university's chancellor) or cathedral or hospital, who resolves disputes, gives ceremonial speeches, etc.
The object in the visitor pattern that performs an operation on the elements of a structure one by one.
To engage the attention of; to awaken interest in; to excite emotion or passion in, in behalf of a person or thing.
definitions.example: Action films don't really interest me.

definitions.definition: To be concerned with or engaged in; to affect; to concern; to excite.
To cause or permit to share.
Of concern; affecting, important.
Arousing or holding the attention or interest of someone.
Pregnant.
The body with the power to make and/or enforce laws to control a country, land area, people or organization.
(grammar) The relationship between a word and its dependents.
The state and its administration viewed as the ruling political power.
definitions.example: If the citizens must follow the law, then the government must follow the constitution.

definitions.definition: The management or control of a system.
The tenure of a chief of state.
A doorlike structure outside a house.
Doorway, opening, or passage in a fence or wall.
Movable barrier.
definitions.example: The gate in front of the railroad crossing went up after the train had passed.

definitions.definition: A logical pathway made up of switches which turn on or off. Examples are and, or, nand, etc.
The gap between a batsman's bat and pad.
definitions.example: Singh was bowled through the gate, a very disappointing way for a world-class batsman to get out.

definitions.definition: The amount of money made by selling tickets to a concert or a sports event.
(flow cytometry) A line that separates particle type-clusters on two-dimensional dot plots.
Passageway (as in an air terminal) where passengers can embark or disembark.
The controlling terminal of a field effect transistor (FET).
In a lock tumbler, the opening for the stump of the bolt to pass through or into.
The channel or opening through which metal is poured into the mould; the ingate.
The waste piece of metal cast in the opening; a sprue or sullage piece. Also written geat and git.
A mechanism, in a film camera and projector, that holds each frame momentarily stationary behind the aperture.
A tally mark consisting of four vertical bars crossed by a diagonal, representing a count of five.
To keep something inside by means of a closed gate.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by not allowing them to go out.
To open a closed ion channel.
To furnish with a gate.
To turn (an image intensifier) on and off selectively as needed, or to avoid damage. See autogating.
To cause a horse or similar mount to speed up.
(by extension) To start moving or move faster; to get a move on.
(directed at a horse) Move on!, go faster!
To pelt with stones, especially to kill by pelting with stones.
definitions.example: She got stoned to death after they found her.

definitions.definition: To wall with stones.
To remove a stone from (fruit etc.).
To form a stone during growth, with reference to fruit etc.
To intoxicate, especially with narcotics. (Usually in passive)
To do nothing, to stare blankly into space and not pay attention when relaxing or when bored.
To lap with an abrasive stone to remove surface irregularities.
Containing stones.
Having had the stones removed.
Drunk; intoxicated by alcohol.
High on drugs, especially cannabis.
definitions.example: We got stoned and ate four bags of potato chips.

definitions.definition: Exhilarated, intoxicated by something (such as love) other than drugs or alcohol.
To make holy; to consecrate; to set aside for sacred or ceremonial use.
To free from sin; to purify.
To make acceptable or useful under religious law or practice.
To endorse with religious sanction.
The science or the technique used to make a classification.
A classification; especially, a classification in a hierarchical system.
The science of finding, describing, classifying and naming organisms.
An artificial lens, especially one of a pair.
A monocle.
An eyepiece.
The lens of the eye.
Punishment inflicted in the spirit of moral outrage or personal vengeance.
To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.
To encounter or discover something being searched for; to locate.
definitions.example: I found my car keys. They were under the couch.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To discover by study or experiment direct to an object or end.
definitions.example: Water is found to be a compound substance.

definitions.definition: To gain, as the object of desire or effort.
definitions.example: to find leisure; to find means

definitions.definition: To attain to; to arrive at; to acquire.
definitions.example: Looks like he found a new vehicle for himself!

definitions.definition: To point out.
definitions.example: He kept finding faults with my work.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To decide that, to discover that, to form the opinion that.
definitions.example: I find your argument unsatisfactory.

definitions.definition: To arrive at, as a conclusion; to determine as true; to establish.
definitions.example: to find a verdict; to find a true bill (of indictment) against an accused person

definitions.definition: To supply; to furnish.
definitions.example: to find food for workmen

definitions.definition: To provide for
definitions.example: He finds his nephew in money.

definitions.definition: To determine or judge.
definitions.example: The jury finds for the defendant.

definitions.definition: To discover game.
A result of research or an investigation.
A formal conclusion by a judge, jury or regulatory agency on issues of fact.
That which is found, a find, a discovery.
The act of discovering something by chance, an instance of finding something by chance.
(generally plural) Tools or materials used in shoe making or repair.
A self-contained component of assembled jewellery.
A fabric or structure of fibrous elements attached to each other at regular intervals.
Any interconnected group or system
definitions.example: A network of roads crisscrossed the country.

definitions.definition: A directory of people maintained for their advancement
definitions.example: To get a job in today's economy, it is important to have a strong network.

definitions.definition: A group of affiliated television stations that broadcast common programs from a parent company.
Multiple computers and other devices connected together to share information
definitions.example: The copy machine is connected to the network so it can now serve as a printer.

definitions.definition: To interact socially for the purpose of getting connections or personal advancement.
definitions.example: Many people find it worthwhile to network for jobs and information.

definitions.definition: To connect two or more computers or other computerized devices.
definitions.example: If we network his machine to the server, he will be able to see all the files.

definitions.definition: To interconnect a group or system.
To broadcast across an entire network of stations and affiliates at the same time.
Lower; under.
definitions.example: The disappointed child’s nether lip quivered.

definitions.definition: Lying beneath, or conceived as lying beneath, the Earth’s surface.
definitions.example: the nether regions

definitions.definition: Down; downward.
Low; low down.
A type of black glass produced by volcanoes.
Black
An animal which is able to consume both plants (like a herbivore) and meat (like a carnivore).
definitions.example: Bears are omnivores: they can eat plants, but they also eat fish.

definitions.definition: A house or shelter for a dog.
A facility at which dogs are reared or boarded.
definitions.example: She raises registered Dalmatians at her kennel.

definitions.definition: (collective) The dogs kept at such a facility; a pack of hounds.
The hole of a fox or other animal.
To house or board a dog (or less commonly another animal).
definitions.example: While we're away our friends will kennel our pet poodle.

definitions.definition: To lie or lodge; to dwell, as a dog or a fox.
To drive (a fox) to covert in its hole.
To concentrate on a particular area of study as a student in a college or university
definitions.example: I have decided to major in mathematics.

definitions.definition: A species of mallow, Althaea officinalis, that grows in marshy terrain.

A type of confectionery, originally (since Ancient Egyptian times) made from this plant, but now generally made of sugar or corn syrup, gelatin that has been pre-softened in water, gum arabic, flavorings, and sometimes beaten egg whites, all whipped to a spongy consistency.
Someone who is soft and benign.
definitions.example: He acts like a total badass, but he's really just a marshmallow once you get to know him.

definitions.definition: (usually capitalized) The garden where Plato taught.
(usually capitalized) Plato's philosophical system based on skepticism; Plato's followers.
An institution for the study of higher learning; a college or a university; typically a private school.
A school or place of training in which some special art is taught.
definitions.example: the military academy at West Point; a riding academy; the Academy of Music.; a music academy; a language academy

definitions.definition: A society of learned people united for the advancement of the arts and sciences, and literature, or some particular art or science.
The knowledge disseminated in an Academy.
(with the, without reference to any specific academy) Academia.
A body of established opinion in a particular field, regarded as authoritative.
A school directly funded by central government, independent of local control.
To entertain with wine.
To drink wine.
To conquer, defeat.
To reach some destination or object, despite difficulty or toil (now usually intransitive, with preposition or locative adverb).
To triumph or achieve victory in (a game, a war, etc.).
To gain (a prize) by succeeding in competition or contest.
definitions.example: to win the jackpot in a lottery;  to win a bottle of wine in a raffle

definitions.definition: To obtain (someone) by wooing; to make an ally or friend of (frequently with over).
To achieve victory.
definitions.example: Who would win in a fight between an octopus and a dolphin?

definitions.definition: To have power, coercion or control.
definitions.example: Ever since the 2013 Boston Marathon bombing, Bostonians now run as "One Boston." The terrorists did not win.

definitions.definition: To obtain (something desired).
definitions.example: The company hopes to win an order from the government worth over 5 million dollars.

definitions.definition: To cause a victory for someone.
definitions.example: The policy success should win the elections for Mr. Smith.

definitions.definition: To extract (ore, coal, etc.).
An individual victory.
definitions.example: Our first win of the season put us in high spirits.

definitions.definition: A feat carried out successfully; a victorious achievement.
Gain; profit; income.
Wealth; goods owned.
Pleasure; joy; delight.
A burning pain in the chest that is caused by stomach acid entering the gullet.
One who exercises control and direction of a military or naval organization.
A naval officer whose rank is above that of a lieutenant commander and below that of captain.
One who exercises control and direction over a group of persons.
A designation or rank in certain non-military organizations such as NASA and various police forces.
The chief officer of a commandry.
A heavy beetle or wooden mallet, used in paving, in sail lofts, etc.
A rank within an honorary order: e.g. Commander of the Legion of Honour.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the Asian genus Moduza.
A diary or daily record of a person, organization, vessel etc.; daybook.
A newspaper or magazine dealing with a particular subject.
definitions.example: The university's biology department subscribes to half a dozen academic journals.

definitions.definition: A chronological record of payments.
A chronological record of changes made to a database or other system; along with a backup or image copy that allows recovery after a failure or reinstatement to a previous time; a log.
The part of a shaft or axle that rests on bearings.
To archive or record something.
To scrapbook.
To insert (a shaft, etc.) in a journal bearing.
Daily.
The mark of the foot left on the earth.
(by extension) A faint mark or visible sign left by something which is lost, or has perished, or is no longer present.
definitions.example: the vestiges of ancient magnificence in Palmyra

definitions.definition: A vestigial organ; a non-functional organ or body part that was once functional in an evolutionary ancestor.
An incandescent lamp or bulb
Emitting light as a result of being heated
Shining very brightly
Showing intense emotion, as of a performance, etc.
A piece of source code or other content that is dynamically retrieved for inclusion in another item.
To bring into a group, class, set, or total as a (new) part or member.
definitions.example: I will purchase the vacation package if you will include car rental.

definitions.definition: To contain, as parts of a whole; to comprehend.
definitions.example: Does this volume of Shakespeare include his sonnets?

definitions.definition: To enclose, confine.
To conclude; to terminate.
To use a directive that allows the use of source code from another file.
definitions.example: You have to include the strings library to use this function.

definitions.definition: To go; walk; proceed.
